# Welcome {#sec-welcome}

This book serves as an A to Z resource for beginners learning to use oTree to 
conduct online behavioral experiments and surveys involving human 
subjects. No prior programming experience is 
assumed. It is designed to accompany ECON 227B Experimental 
Economics II lectures and course materials. The detailed syllabus 
and other course materials are available on the Canvas page for 
this course.

In this book, you will be introduced to coding simple experiments 
in the form of "applications" using oTree and deploying them on 
the Heroku server using oTree Hub. We will begin by installing 
oTree and Visual Studio Code on your personal laptops and 
familiarizing ourselves with the VS Code environment. Topics 
covered include creating an oTree application, executing common 
oTree tasks, testing code on a local server, running a session 
with human subjects, coding best practices, and using AI 
effectively to build your code. This book will also provide a 
curated list of resources by oTree creators and other users that 
you can draw on for your individual projects. The point is not to 
reinvent the wheel, but to stand on the shoulders of giants!

## Prerequisites

Familiarity with Python is helpful but not required — we will 
build everything from scratch together. If you have never written 
a line of code before, do not worry. By the end of this course 
you will be comfortable navigating a coding environment and 
building your own experiments.

## A Note on This Book

This book will grow alongside the course. Links to reference code and 
additional materials will be added as we progress through each 
session. If you are browsing ahead and find session pages that 
appear empty or incomplete, that is expected — content will be 
added as we reach those sessions in class.
